text: Lipids are important molecules for efficient energy storage, in addition to roles as signaling molecules and basic building blocks in organisms.  In addition to energy release, lipid metabolism affects the amount of stored fat.  Alteration of lipid metabolism reflects a disruption of normal function, as evidenced by changes in gene expression, enzyme levels, break-down products, or fat content.  Peroxisome proliferation-activated receptors pathways (and associated genes and proteins) are commonly monitored for downstream effects on lipid metabolism (Luquet et al. 2005; Den Broeder et al. 2015; Chamorro-Garcia et al. 2018; Venezia et al. 2021).
